text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should learn how to make a sourdough starter because it's the foundation of almost any good bread. Then, you'll need to find the right ratio of flour and water and wait patiently for the wild yeast to develop naturally. Next, you should look into the cosmic expansion of the universe because it's a mind-blowing concept that really changes how you look at the world. Speaking of which, did you know that galaxies are actually moving away from each other at an accelerating rate? Anyway, Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that long fermentation period where the magic happens and the dough becomes light and airy. This is also when you develop that complex flavor profile you're looking for. After, once the dough is proofed and shaped, you'll want to chill it in the fridge overnight, which also helps to develop the crust. Finally, when you're ready to bake, make sure you preheat your oven and the Dutch oven for at least 45 minutes to get that perfect, crispy crust. And to finish the loaf off properly, the last thing to do is let it cool completely on a wire rack
